articles.title: ‘Toy Story 5’ VFX Supervisor Says ‘Decoy Version’ of Film Was Made to Hide Taylor Swift’s Involvement
articles.why: The revelation that Pixar created an entirely fake decoy version of Toy Story 5 — complete enough to fool their own production crew — to protect Taylor Swift's secret involvement is one of the most audacious acts of Hollywood secrecy in the streaming era.
articles.hook: Toy Story 5's VFX crew worked on a completely fake version of the film for months so nobody would find out Taylor Swift was making a song for it.

articles.title: Google will pay SpaceX $920 million a month to use xAI's data centers
articles.why: Google paying SpaceX $920 million per month for compute capacity — because its own infrastructure couldn't handle AI demand — is one of the most startling infrastructure deals in tech history and a concrete signal of how broken the AI supply chain is.
articles.hook: Google's AI products generated so much demand that it had to pay SpaceX $920 million a month just to keep up — using xAI's data centers.

articles.title: Phoebe Bridgers Is Going on Tour
articles.why: Phoebe Bridgers announcing her first official solo tour in three years — with Alex G and Isaac Wood as openers — is the formal confirmation that her offline, mystery-building rollout has now converted into a full concert campaign. This is the moment her return becomes real.
articles.hook: Phoebe Bridgers just announced her first solo tour in three years, with Alex G and Isaac Wood opening — and tickets will almost certainly be impossible to get.

articles.title: Hear TS (Taylor Swift) Tackle TS (Toy Story 5)
articles.why: Taylor Swift writing a country song for Toy Story 5 and breaking Spotify streaming records for country music is a concrete data point about her commercial reach — and it lands at the intersection of two of the summer's biggest cultural stories.
articles.hook: Taylor Swift wrote a country song for Toy Story 5 and it just broke Spotify's record for the most-streamed country song in a single day.

articles.title: Google and FBI warn of ransomware group that sends fake IT workers to hack victims in person
articles.why: A ransomware group sending fake IT workers physically into law firm offices to steal data via USB drives is a genuinely new and alarming escalation in cybercrime — moving from digital attacks to in-person infiltration.
articles.hook: A ransomware gang is now sending people disguised as IT support workers into law firm offices to plug in USB drives and steal data in person.

articles.title: NASA briefly sheltered space station astronauts in SpaceX’s Dragon due to leaks
articles.why: ISS astronauts sheltering in a SpaceX Dragon capsule due to new Russian module leaks is the most concrete safety escalation in the ongoing space station story — this is now a live human safety situation.
articles.hook: Five astronauts had to take shelter inside the SpaceX Dragon capsule after Roscosmos discovered new air leaks in the Russian service module.

articles.title: Safety officials finally have a good idea of what a big rocket explosion can do
articles.why: The Blue Origin explosion producing the first real-world data on what a large rocket detonation does to a commercial spaceport is genuinely useful for an industry planning hundreds more launches — this is science coming from disaster.
articles.hook: The Blue Origin explosion gave safety officials their first real data on what a full-scale rocket detonation does to a modern spaceport — and it's reshaping how future launches get approved.

articles.title: Devin Booker Debuts The Nike Book 2 “Tiger Camo”
articles.why: Devin Booker's Nike Book 2 debut is a live product moment — the Tiger Camo colorway revealed at Nike HQ signals bold creative direction for one of the NBA's most style-conscious players' signature line.
articles.hook: Devin Booker revealed the Nike Book 2 Tiger Camo at Nike's own headquarters, and it might be the wildest basketball shoe colorway of the year.

articles.title: Shivani Pandya Malhotra Steps Down as Managing Director of Saudi Arabia’s Red Sea Film Festival (EXCLUSIVE)
articles.why: The departure of the Red Sea Film Festival's founding MD — the architect of one of the Arab world's most ambitious cultural institutions — raises real questions about whether Saudi Arabia's cultural soft power project is losing its independent creative identity.
articles.hook: The woman who built Saudi Arabia's flagship film festival from nothing just walked out, and nobody's explaining why.

articles.title: The US Has a Plan to Combat Screwworm. It Involves a Lot More Flies
articles.why: The US government's plan to fight the flesh-eating screwworm outbreak — by releasing millions of sterilized flies — is a genuinely strange and specific biosecurity response to a confirmed threat in South Texas, and the capacity gap makes this a live concern.
articles.hook: The US plan to stop the flesh-eating screwworm invasion is to flood the area with millions of sterile flies — but the country currently can't produce nearly enough of them.

articles.title: Obsession Knows What the TikTok Generation Fears Most
articles.why: Obsession is one of the summer's genuine box office surprises, and The Atlantic's cultural analysis — framing its horror around Gen Z's specific anxieties about vulnerability and digital exposure — adds real context to why it's resonating.
articles.hook: The Atlantic argues Obsession hit because it tapped into Gen Z's deepest fear — not monsters, but the terror of being truly seen by someone.
